M-069.NAME: brand-assets-favicon-email-logo
M-069.path: frontend/public frontend/index.html frontend/vite.config.ts frontend-admin/public frontend-admin/index.html backend/app/email README.md scripts/phase51-brand-assets-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-51.xml
M-069.owns: Browser favicon/PWA assets and the public logo rendered inside KrotPN Resend HTML emails.
M-069.purpose: Provide a consistent KrotPN browser identity and branded email body logo without changing the current visible frontend UI layout.

exports.asset-user-favicon: Serve favicon.ico plus 16x16/32x32 PNG fallbacks from the user frontend static build.
exports.asset-admin-favicon: Serve favicon.ico plus 16x16/32x32 PNG fallbacks from the admin frontend static build.
exports.asset-pwa-icons: Serve apple-touch-icon.png, pwa-192x192.png, and pwa-512x512.png from the user frontend build.
exports.asset-email-logo: Serve the email body logo at /brand/email-logo.png from the user frontend static build.
exports.fn-email-logo-rendering: Render the public email-logo URL in verification and password-reset HTML templates while keeping text fallbacks clean.
exports.smoke-phase51-brand-assets: Validate assets, dimensions, HTML references, email template binding, README notes, and protected deploy surfaces.

constraints.constraint-1: Do not add visible logo components to the current website, user cabinet, or admin panel; that belongs to the future frontend redesign phase.
constraints.constraint-2: Do not change Resend API payload semantics beyond the HTML content already sent through M-040.
constraints.constraint-3: Do not log verification/reset tokens, logo URLs with tokens, API keys, or provider secrets.
constraints.constraint-4: Do not change install.sh, deploy scripts, docker-compose, nginx edge topology, VPN runtime, MTProto runtime, or BIMI DNS behavior.
constraints.constraint-5: Email body logo display is mailbox-client dependent because many clients block remote images until the recipient allows images.

acceptance-metrics.metric-1: User and admin builds include working favicon.ico plus 16x16/32x32 PNG fallbacks.
acceptance-metrics.metric-2: User frontend includes 180x180 apple-touch-icon.png, 192x192 PWA icon, 512x512 PWA icon, and a 256x256 public email-logo PNG.
acceptance-metrics.metric-3: Email templates render src="{FRONTEND_URL}/brand/email-logo.png" in HTML only, with alt text and no logo URL in text fallback.
acceptance-metrics.metric-4: Phase-51 smoke and backend email tests fail if the logo/template/asset contract drifts.

verification-design.evidence-level-1: Static asset smoke checks ICO signature, PNG signatures, expected dimensions, frontend/admin HTML links, Vite PWA asset names, README notes, and protected deploy diff.
verification-design.evidence-level-2: Backend email tests verify verification/reset HTML contains the public logo URL and text fallbacks remain token-focused without logo noise.
verification-design.evidence-level-3: Frontend and admin production builds prove public assets copy into dist without adding visible UI logo surfaces.
verification-design.evidence-level-4: Optional live curl after clean deploy verifies https://krotpn.xyz/favicon.ico and https://krotpn.xyz/brand/email-logo.png return the expected static assets.

execution-evidence.evidence-1: 2026-06-02: node scripts/phase51-brand-assets-smoke.mjs passed for source assets, HTML references, email template binding, README notes, and protected deploy/install diff guard.
execution-evidence.evidence-2: 2026-06-02: backend/tests/test_email_delivery.py passed 5/5 with public email-logo HTML assertions and unchanged text fallback/token redaction behavior.
execution-evidence.evidence-3: 2026-06-02: frontend npm run build and frontend-admin npm run build passed; node scripts/phase51-brand-assets-smoke.mjs --expect-dist confirmed Vite copied favicon/PWA/email-logo assets into dist.
execution-evidence.evidence-4: 2026-06-02: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and git diff --check passed.

M-069.hotspot: H-056: Favicon and email-logo assets must remain public static branding only, while BIMI/provider avatar rendering stays a separate mailbox-provider-controlled surface
